body:
## Summary

After an agentic run via `formal-ai with <cli>` completes, there is **no line telling the user where the agentic harness's session/transcript file is** for debugging. Each wrapped CLI writes a session log (codex: `codex resume <id>`; gemini: `gemini --resume <id>`; qwen/opencode/claude keep their own transcripts), but `formal-ai with` doesn't surface the path.

## Request

When a `formal-ai with <tool>` invocation finishes (both interactive exit and non-interactive), print a final line pointing to the session file(s), e.g.:

```
formal-ai: session transcript for debugging:
  codex:     ~/.codex/sessions/019f70d1-...jsonl   (resume: codex resume 019f70d1-...)
  formal-ai: ~/.formal-ai/logs/<run-id>.jsonl       (if server-side logging is on)
```

Details:
- Resolve the wrapped CLI's session/transcript location per tool (codex/gemini/qwen/opencode/agent/grok/cursor; claude excluded from the sweep) and print it.
- If the Formal AI server also logs the run (e.g. via `formal-ai proxy` or a server session log), print that path too, so both sides can be inspected.
- Keep it one concise, copy-pasteable block at the end of the run.

## Note (not a bug): `hi` running `Bash(date)` in the Claude TUI

I checked the "why does `hi` run `date`" question. At the Formal AI API, `hi` does **not** misroute — with a `Bash`/`shell`/`exec_command` tool advertised, `hi` returns `"Hi, how may I help you?"` and emits no tool call. The `Bash(date)` seen in the Claude Code screenshot is **Claude Code's own startup environment-gathering** (it runs `date`/git status at session start), independent of Formal AI. So this is not a Formal AI routing bug. A session-path line (this issue) would make such harness-side actions easier to attribute.

## Environment
formal-ai 0.297.1, macOS arm64.


---
## Implementation spec — known per-tool session locations
Print at end of run (resolve the concrete path/id):
- codex: `~/.codex/sessions/<uuid>.jsonl` (+ `codex resume <uuid>`)
- gemini: session under `~/.gemini/` (+ `gemini --resume <id>`)
- qwen: `~/.qwen/…`
- opencode: `~/.local/share/opencode/…` (+ `opencode --session <id>`)
- claude: `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/…jsonl` (but with the temp `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` the wrapper sets, it's under that temp dir — print the temp path)
- agent (@link-assistant/agent): its transcript dir
- grok: `~/.grok/…`
Also print the Formal AI server log path if proxy/logging enabled. Emit one block:
```
formal-ai: session files for debugging:
  <tool>:     <path>   (resume: <cmd>)
  server log: <path>
```
title: Print the agentic harness session/transcript file path after a 'formal-ai with' run finishes (for debugging) (v0.297.1)
